summary: Iter197: Verified the new 'Fix Missing Piece Definitions' feature end-to-end. Backend GET /api/manufactured-products/missing-piece-def and POST /api/manufactured-products/fix-piece-definitions are working correctly (6/6 pytest tests pass): scan returns the seeded corrupt product 'rab-missing-demo' (40g recipe, شريحة unit, piece_def_value=0); batch fix with piece_def_value=80, piece_def_unit=غرام updates the record and the product's computed_yield becomes exactly 0.5 (40/80). Invalid value (<=0), non-weight unit (حبة), and foreign tenant ids are correctly placed in the 'skipped' array. Re-scan after a successful fix excludes the now-defined product. Frontend (route /warehouse-manufacturing, tab 'التصنيع') flow also works: red 'إصلاح التعريفات المفقودة' button opens the dialog, dialog lists 'دجاج راب (يحتاج تعريف)' with input data-testid='missing-def-value-rab-missing-demo', filling 80 and clicking 'حفظ الكل' produces success toast 'تم إصلاح 1 منتج', dialog closes, and a second re-open immediately shows the empty-state message 'ممتاز! لا توجد منتجات تنقصها تعريفات'.
critical_code_review_comments: fix_piece_definitions endpoint correctly enforces tenant isolation by adding tenant_id to the update filter; foreign/non-existent ids fall into 'skipped' rather than silently succeeding.

get_missing_piece_definitions correctly excludes products whose main unit is already a weight unit (in _UNIT_WEIGHT_MAP) and those with a real piece_weight > 1 (so the legitimate الـ piece_weight path still works as alternative truth source).
Both endpoints are registered before the dynamic /manufactured-products/{product_id} route in inventory_system.py (lines 4019 & 4066) — confirmed they are reachable without route collision.

Minor (non-blocking): error responses inside skipped[] are returned as free-text Arabic strings ('قيمة أو وحدة غير صالحة', 'غير موجود أو لا يخص هذا المستأجر'). Consider adding a stable error_code field (e.g. 'INVALID_VALUE', 'NOT_FOUND') so frontends can switch on it without string matching.

success_rate.frontend: 100% (login → /warehouse-manufacturing → tab-manufacturing → fix-missing-piece-def-btn → dialog → fill 80 → save → toast + dialog close → re-scan shows empty list)

seed_data_creation: Used the existing seeded demo product 'rab-missing-demo' (tenant=default, unit شريحة, piece_weight=1غرام, piece_def_value=0, recipe=40g دجاج). The pytest fixture (reset_demo_before_each) resets piece_def_value back to 0 and piece_def_unit to None before AND after every test, so the suite is idempotent regardless of run order and leaves DB in the original 'needs fixing' state for the main agent.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The two new endpoints live in /app/backend/routes/inventory_system.py at lines 4019 (GET missing-piece-def) and 4066 (POST fix-piece-definitions). Re-seed the demo product to its 'broken' state at any time with: db.manufactured_products.update_one({'id':'rab-missing-demo'},{'\$set':{'piece_def_value':0,'piece_def_unit':None}}). The frontend button is hidden until tab data-testid='tab-manufacturing' is active (it lives in the activeTab==='manufacturing' branch in WarehouseManufacturing.js around line 2305). Selectors used and verified: fix-missing-piece-def-btn, missing-piece-def-dialog, missing-def-value-{id}, missing-def-unit-{id}, missing-def-save-btn.
rca of the issue: Not a bug-fix iteration — this is a new feature verification. The feature itself correctly addresses the underlying root cause: previously, manufactured products with a count main unit (e.g. شريحة) whose piece_def_value was missing/0 were falling back to a 1-gram placeholder weight, making computed_yield = recipe_grams / 1 (e.g. 40 instead of the correct 40/80 = 0.5). The new scan endpoint surfaces exactly that population (count unit + recipe grams > 0 + no valid piece_def AND no real piece_weight>1), and the batch fix sets a proper piece_def_value so _enrich_unit_cost_fields then yields the correct half-portion ratio. Verified on the seeded rab-missing-demo product: after POST with piece_def_value=80, GET /api/manufactured-products returns computed_yield=0.5 exactly.
